sentences.text: Did you guys have a good uh New Year's?
I think I was in bed by maybe 10 o'clock.
Take out in a movie uh with my girlfriend,
so that was good enough for me.
Yeah, we watched the ball drop Eastern time or Central, so 11 o'clock, you know, kiss the husband and boys, and I was like, I'm off to bed.
Uh, as you guys are, are, I'm sure you're aware, uh, I'm gonna take over for Adam, um, but I've been with the company for about 2 years, so, uh, well versed in the space.
I've been pretty much caught up by, uh, Corey and Justin, um.
So if there's anything that I might miss, please let me know, but uh I believe you all just got done piloting us, so that's the reason for the call today.
I totally forgot we only got you for two months.
We can get you signed up real quick if that's right,
but yeah, I'd love to get kind of uh some feedback for you and then kind of just go over the notes that I, I have from Corey and Justin.
I mean, I used it quite a bit too.
Did you use it at all?
I was in there quite a bit.
Yeah, I mean, it's like very user friendly, so, and I love that, you know, the data is up to date, so and there's a
No work for the on-site team, so that's really great too.
Easy to just pull the information when we needed it.
And then remind me, I believe you guys are currently using radix, is that right?
yeah, so we've, I'm sure, I'm sure the team's gone over, but we have transitioned probably about 1.5 million of their like paying units over here.
So, uh, this would be a pretty seamless transition for you all.
I mean, even just the ones we onboarded were so easy.
It's just like, OK, let's take a look at the comps and Had to add a few comps.
So, I mean, yeah, it was really nice.
I used the research quite a bit.
I think Kiana did too is we kind of onboard new communities to take a look at their market ahead of time.
So that's, I mean, I love that portion of it, so.
Awesome, yeah, and then, uh, I mean, we're gonna have uh probably some more additions coming out to uh the research functionality here pretty soon, so we'll definitely keep you in the loop of that.
But then as far as kind of timeline and numbers go, correct me if I'm wrong, um, I think you guys are looking for an April start date.
I, I think radix, we have radix until the end of March, so we'd have to put the notice here in pretty soon.
I do want to touch back with our um our COO and our, we have two SVPs of The communities and just see what their thoughts are.
Um, they had access to, but they don't use it as much as we do, but I know one of them did.

So, um, yeah, we would just need to touch base with them and see if they had any other specific questions as far as reporting and making sure we've got the information that we could get from Rodd too.
Um, so I guess if we if we need to have like another call with them, let me know.

Uh, otherwise, I think, uh, I guess, correct me if I'm wrong, but if you can get the approval from them, I think we aligned on 40 cents a unit, uh, we can get you up and running, um, as fast as possible and just delay your first invoice till April 1st.
OK, and then um at the 40 cents per unit per month, if we onboard a certain number, does that go down?
Do you guys have like a tiered pricing program?
I think the uh I think the 40 cents was quoted for, I think you guys have about 10,000 units, is that correct?
Yeah, so I think if you go above 10,000, let me pull
Well, we don't, we don't have more than 10,000,
so yeah, so you can look at like a flat rate of like 40 cents then.

Um, yeah, so I guess, uh, as far as the timeline goes for us to, to reconnect, um, I always hate being an annoying sales rep, so I kind of like to defer to you all when when would make sense for me to reach back out to potentially schedule another call.
Um, let's talk the last week of February, so like the week of the 24th.
So if anything comes up before then or you need my assistance or any additional kind of collateral, let me know, um, or if we need to meet before then just send me an email and.
